strategy: Buy $87 Call — Aug 2026 (post-Q1 beat, clean 87-day window to next earnings)

wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. However, CL's defensive profile and post-earnings clarity make this one of the safer wheel entries at current VIX. Low IV (~26%) means premiums are modest but assignment risk is low on a 63-year dividend grower. Can enter with reduced size now; add on VIX > 20.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart: stock pulled back from 52-week high of $99.33 to $85.37 (-14%). Lower Bollinger Band estimated ~$80–83. Post-earnings (Q1 beat May 1) the stock is in a clean recovery phase. Current price is mid-to-lower band — a Bollinger Band entry signal is approaching. CSP entry at $78–82 is at or below lower band, ideal for mean-reversion thesis.

csp.annualisedYield: ~15–25% annualised on capital at risk (lower IV but ultra-stable underlying)
csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. CRITICAL: Close before July 31 earnings (Q2 2026). Re-enter after. Dividend ex-date watch: quarterly $0.53 dividend — factor into CSP timing to capture dividend if assigned.
csp.note: ⭐ IDEAL DEFENSIVE WHEEL STOCK — 63 years of consecutive dividend increases, #1 global toothpaste brand, $20.5B annual revenue. IV ~26% means premiums are modest but assignment at $78–82 gives excellent cost basis vs. analyst targets ($96 avg). Capital at risk: $7,800–$8,200/contract (fits a standard retail portfolio at 1 contract). If assigned, you own a dividend-paying Dividend Aristocrat at a 9–14% discount to current price.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium. Re-sell monthly. Collect dividend while holding shares. Close before July 31 earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned on CSP (cost basis ~$78–82), sell CC at $87–90 — just above current market price. Low IV keeps premiums modest but the dividend (2.47% yield) supplements the wheel return. Total return: CSP premium + dividend + CC premium = solid defensive income.

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter after Q1 earnings IV crush has settled (already reported May 1). Enter now or on any dip toward $83–84.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 60–90% gain OR when stock hits $92–94 (first target). Let 1 contract ride to $96–99 for 130–180% gain.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not reached $89+ by ~June 20 (50% of time elapsed from entry), close the position. Do not fight accelerating theta in the final 30 days.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on premium paid (~$1.75–2.25 loss per share). Do not hold a decaying position hoping for a reversal.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by July 26 (5 days before July 31 Q2 earnings) — no exceptions for long premium positions.

options.bestFor: Only if stock moves strongly within 4 weeks. Theta brutal at 21 days left on exit.
Sweet spot — 56 days of time value at exit, can hold through earnings if Q2 looks clean.
Holding through two earnings cycles. Overkill for 12% upside target — overpaying for unused time.

recommendation.title: Single-Leg Call — Post-Q1 Beat, Low IV Means No Spread Needed, Clean Entry Window
recommendation.type: Call (single leg — IV ~26% is below 40% spread threshold; naked call is appropriate)
recommendation.entryNote: POST-EARNINGS CLEAN ENTRY: Q1 2026 reported May 1 — beat on EPS ($0.97 vs $0.94 est) and revenue ($5.32B vs $5.22B est). Full-year guidance reaffirmed. IV crush has already happened — premiums are now fair. Next earnings July 31 (~87 days away), so Aug expiry captures the full pre-earnings recovery window. LOW IV (~26%) means a single-leg call is appropriate — NO spread required per the IV rule (spreads are for IV > 60%). 2 contracts = $700–1,000 — within a typical ~$700/position budget. Bull thesis: stock has pulled back 14% from $99 52-week high on tariff/margin fears; Q1 beat shows those fears are partially priced in; analyst average target $96 implies 12% upside from here. JPMorgan raised to $96 and Rothschild upgraded to Buy on May 4. Increasing options volume post-earnings signals institutional positioning ahead of recovery.

exitPlan.label: Contract 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $92–94 (+8–10%) on continued defensive rotation or analyst upgrades

exitPlan.label: Contract 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $96–99 (+12–16%) on analyst target convergence or margin recovery signal

companyOverview: Colgate-Palmolive is the world's largest oral care company and a quintessential Dividend Aristocrat — 63 consecutive years of dividend increases, uninterrupted payments since 1895. Its four business pillars are Oral Care (toothpaste, toothbrushes, mouthwash — ~41.5% global toothpaste market share), Personal Care, Home Care, and Hill's Pet Nutrition (premium pet food, high margin). 70% of revenue is generated outside the US, with ~45% from emerging markets where Colgate has dominant local brand positions (e.g., >70% toothpaste share in Brazil). The stock pulled back from $99 to $85 on tariff headwinds and margin compression fears — then Q1 2026 (reported May 1) beat expectations on both EPS and revenue with full-year guidance reaffirmed, and increasing options volume signals the market is positioning for recovery. The Strategic Growth & Productivity Program (expanded April 30) targets $200–300M in annual savings once fully implemented, with the tariff burden ($300M incremental headwind) expected to peak in 2026 before moderating.

REAFFIRMED post-Q1. Gross margin guidance revised lower (tariff/raw material headwind ~$300M)

Q2 2026 — 87 days away; Aug expiry options clear earnings

Elevated leverage (typical for consumer staples with predictable cash flows). Not acute risk given FCF.

insiderFlow.summary: Net SELLING by top executives in February at higher prices ($93–94). Not alarming — pre-planned and stock has since dropped 9%. RSU vesting activity and deferred comp grants dominate recent filings. No open-market buying. Neutral-to-slightly-negative signal but not a red flag at current depressed price.

transactions.note: Sold at $93.91 — stock now $85.37, so CEO sold 9% higher than current price. Pre-planned. Not a current negative signal.
Coordinated same-day sale with CEO. Both under 10b5-1 plans. Sold at near 52-week high — good timing but pre-planned.
Performance-based RSU vesting — compensation, not discretionary activity.

insiderFlow.signal: NEUTRAL — CEO and CFO sold at $93–94 in February under 10b5-1 plans (stock is now lower, so they exited near the top pre-crash). No open-market buying at current depressed prices. Insider ownership is low (~0.3%) which is typical for large-cap consumer staples.

catalysts: Q1 2026 beat (EPS +3.2%, revenue +1.9%) with guidance reaffirmed — market overshoot on tariff fears is reversing
Increasing options volume post-earnings signals institutional positioning ahead of recovery
Rothschild Buy upgrade ($100 PT) April 21 + JPMorgan raised to $96 May 4 — analyst upgrade cycle beginning
Dividend Aristocrat premium: 63-year dividend growth streak attracts institutional income mandates at depressed prices
Strategic Growth & Productivity Program expanded (April 30): $200–300M annual savings target — margin recovery catalyst for 2027
Emerging markets resilience: 45% of sales in high-growth markets; Latin America a particularly strong moat
S&P 500 component with high institutional ownership — defensive rotation demand in volatile market environment
14% pullback from 52-week high to $85 creates attractive entry vs. $96 analyst consensus — 12% upside built-in
risks: Tariff headwind: ~$300M incremental raw material/packaging/logistics cost in 2026 — gross margin guidance revised lower after Q1
Operating margin compression: 18.1% vs 21.9% prior year — full recovery depends on productivity program and tariff moderation
High leverage: D/E ratio 147.9 — elevated but manageable given predictable FCF from essential consumer products
FX sensitivity: 70% non-US revenue — strong USD creates translation headwind; Q1 had a +5.1% FX tailwind but this can reverse
Slow growth: organic sales guidance +1–4% — this is not a high-growth name; upside is multiple re-rating, not explosive earnings growth
July 31 earnings: do NOT hold calls through Q2 report — if tariff impact worsens or margin compression accelerates, stock could re-test lows
Microsoft Power Automate... (n/a — wrong stock) — main risk is commodity/raw material cost continuation into H2 2026
dilution: NO DILUTION RISK — mature consumer staples company. Share buyback program ongoing. Dividend growth is funded by operating cash flow. High D/E ratio (147.9) is structural for the business model but not an acute near-term concern.
